reviewing.ea_work: Files 7-13 of Item 2.4 -- closes the entire 2.4 file-scope work

guide_review_response.summary: EA-4 has completed Item 2.4 file scope: files 7 (self_check.py), 8 (blarai_next_task_resolver.py), 9 (cleaner.py with g5-3 closed), and 13 (vikunja_mcp/server.py with full §D.1 hardening) are refactored on disk; files 10 (fleet_ops/diff_builder.py), 11 (autonomy_budget/active_tasks.py), and 12 (fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py) correctly require NO CHANGE in 2.4 scope per the v3 handoff plan (file 12 deferred to 2.6 per g5-4). Independent disk verification confirms every claim reproduces: git log -3 unchanged (zero new commits — single-commit discipline holds); git status shows 4 NEW M files added vs EA-3 close (blarai_next_task_resolver.py +12 lines, self_check.py +19, cleaner.py +44, server.py +63); git stash list shows stash@{0} byte-identical timestamp 20260424_013002; state.json fleet_paused=true with original reason and last_updated_by; pytest tools/ shows EXACTLY 280 passed (matches v0e baseline; zero regression); pytest tools/vikunja_mcp/ shows EXACTLY 26 passed (matches EA-4's claim). The §D.1 enforcement in file 13 is THOROUGH: list_tasks now `(*, project_id: int, ...)` with _require_project_id guard and the unscoped /tasks branch removed (always /projects/{project_id}/tasks); search_tasks now `(*, project_id: int, query: str)` with the previously-default cross-project /tasks?s=... closed (always /projects/{project_id}/tasks?s=...); plus defensive _require_project_id guards on get_project, update_project, delete_project, create_task, bulk_create_tasks (5 additional handlers — chokepoint contract extended consistently beyond bare §D.1 scope as good defensive coding). The lazy ctx resolver design (_ctx module-level cache + _get_project_context() helper) is excellent: avoids import-time _project_context.resolve() which would break unrelated unit tests, while production path resolves on first handler call. EA-4's r2 correction (F-5 tests target label handlers, not list/search signatures) is correct and a valid pushback on my standing reminder. g5-3 closed via Option (b) — project-aware fallback in cleaner.py.run_sweep with three-branch logic preserving config-injected primacy + project_context-derived path + legacy zero-arg fallback for back-compat. Decisions D1-D4 honored throughout. All 27 binding acknowledgments held (no resume_fleet, no commits, no merge, no stash touch, no governance edits). One small structural deferral noted (HTTP routing through VikunjaClient instance methods deferred to 2.6 — EA-4 imported _require_project_id directly rather than wiring the VikunjaClient stub now; defensible since VikunjaClient HTTP delegation is itself 2.6-scope per Stage 2.1 stub design). RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE this session into 2.4.v2 (--help verification + MCP handler routing checks; low-risk and small) and likely 2.5 + 2.5.v2 (PS1 work + critical_notify.ps1 inspection — self-contained scope similar in complexity to one or two Item-2.4 files). RE-EVALUATE budget honestly at the 2.5.v2 → 2.6 boundary; 2.6 is the next big-lift comparable to file 13 (extended grep + chokepoint routing across multiple files + g5-4 closure + D3 closure). If budget is healthy at that boundary, push into 2.6; if even slightly tight, emit v4 mid-stage handoff per i1-4 / dn15 (docs-only commit, no partial-commit refactor).

independent_disk_verification.check: git log --oneline -3 (Guide-5 observed): 9d17e7f docs(platform_separation): Guide instance 4→5 handoff ccdd82b docs(platform_separation): Stage 2 mid-stage EA handoff v3 3b9b1b8 docs(platform_separation): Stage 2 mid-stage EA handoff v2 ZERO new commits since EA-4 began. The 4 newly-modified file 7/8/9/13 changes are all uncommitted in working tree per the Stage-2 single-commit-at-2.9 protocol. Match EA-4's claim "No commits per single-commit-at-2.9 protocol" exactly.

git status --porcelain shows 4 NEW M files vs EA-3 close: M tools/autonomy_budget/blarai_next_task_resolver.py (file 8) M tools/autonomy_budget/self_check.py (file 7) M tools/gate_stale_cleaner/cleaner.py (file 9) M tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py (file 13) git diff --stat line counts: blarai_next_task_resolver.py | 12 (single-line default change + module constant comment block) self_check.py | 19 (kwarg added, conditional config_path derivation, docstring) cleaner.py | 44 (import + kwarg + 3-branch fallback logic + docstring -- substantive g5-3 closure) server.py | 63 (imports + lazy ctx resolver + §D.1 hardening on list_tasks/search_tasks + 5 defensive guards on get/update/delete_project/create_task/bulk_create_tasks + docstring annotations) EXACTLY matches EA-4's claim "4 modified files (files 7, 8, 9, 13)".
git stash list: stash@{0}: On main: wake_launcher auto-stash sdo 20260424_013002 Byte-identical to EA-3 close-out and predecessor v2/v3 ground-truth. Untouched.

state.json: fleet_paused: true fleet_paused_reason: "platform separation v2 in progress" last_updated_by: "copilot_agent" last_updated_utc: "2026-04-24T20:46:12Z" schema_version: 1 No state.resume_fleet() call this session. Original reason and last_updated_by/utc preserved verbatim from EA-2's pause at Stage 2 entry. Acks n=2, n=4, i1-2 all honored.

.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest tools/ --tb=short -q Result: 280 passed in 2.27s EXACT match to v0e baseline (280) from every prior verification. ZERO regression despite 4 substantive refactors including the §D.1 hardening on list_tasks / search_tasks signatures. This confirms no test in the current 280-baseline depended on the old project_id=None default (would have broken on the new (*, project_id: int) keyword-only required signature).

.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest tools/vikunja_mcp/ --tb=short -q Result: 26 passed in 0.80s EXACTLY 26 passes (10 test_bridge + 7 test_multi_writer + 5 test_scheduled_cowork_ea + 4 test_server_f5). EA-4's claim "26/26 pass" verified. EA-4's r2 correction is correct: test_server_f5.py targets label handlers (specifically the F-5 fix from CLAUDE.md history around remove_label_from_task), NOT list_tasks/search_tasks signatures. My standing reminder r2 was over-cautious; EA-4's empirical pushback is valid.

Read tools/autonomy_budget/self_check.py end-to-end: - Import added: `from tools import _project_context` (line 31). ✓ - run() signature: `project_context: "_project_context.ProjectContext | None" = None` kwarg added at line 59 (last position; existing 5 kwargs preserved). ✓ - Conditional derivation at lines 88-91: when config_path is None AND project_context is provided, derive `config_path = project_context.root / "tools" / "autonomy_budget" / "config.yaml"`. ✓ - Existing test invocations (5 tests in test_self_check.py per EA-4's 8/8 pass) UNCHANGED because they pass explicit config_path (which wins). Backwards-compat preserved. ✓ - Library variant pattern (no argparse, no main() change) -- correct per file type "library_no_main" in v3 handoff. ✓

Read tools/autonomy_budget/blarai_next_task_resolver.py: - Import added: `from tools import _project_context` (line 54). ✓ - Constructor default changed: line 102 now reads `repo_root: Path | str = _project_context.DEFAULT_BLARAI_ROOT` (was hardcoded `Path(r"C:/Users/mrbla/BlarAI")`). ✓ - Module-level constant `PROJECT_BLARAI_CORE_DEVELOPMENT: int = 3` PRESERVED unchanged (line 68); annotated with explanatory comment block (lines 61-67) that this is flagged for migration to ctx.vikunja_project_id at Item 2.6, deferred per Master Plan §D.1. Preserves resolver's public class signature stable for 2.4 -- correct deferral scope. ✓ - 12 lines of changes total -- exactly the minimum for the v3 handoff approach. ✓

Read tools/gate_stale_cleaner/cleaner.py: - Import added: `from tools import _project_context` (line 21). ✓ - run_sweep() signature: `project_context: "_project_context.ProjectContext | None" = None` kwarg added at line 171 (after lifts_path). ✓ - Three-branch fallback logic (lines 198-222) replacing the original single-line `cfg = config if config is not None else config_loader.load_config()`: (1) if config is not None: use config (existing config-injected callers unchanged) (2) elif project_context is not None: project-aware load_config(path=ctx.root/..., project_overlay_path=ctx.root/projects/{name.lower()}.yaml) -- the g5-3 Option (b) project-aware fallback (3) else: legacy zero-arg config_loader.load_config() -- back-compat for README example and ad-hoc local usage - Detailed docstring (lines 188-197) explaining Stage 2.4 g5-3 disposition rationale. - Order matters: config-injected primacy preserves existing test invariants (cleaner.py tests pass `config=_CFG` and rely on that taking precedence). ✓ - 6/6 tests pass per EA-4 claim; my pytest tools/ confirms 280-baseline (which includes cleaner tests). ✓ - Correct disposition of g5-3 as 2.4-scope work, not deferred to 2.6. Matches my standing reminder r3 ("g5-3 closes at 2.4 (file 9), g5-4 closes at 2.6 (legacy fold) -- NOT symmetric"). ✓

Read tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py end-to-end:
Imports (lines 24-25): from tools import _project_context from tools._vikunja_client import _require_project_id ✓ Both imports present.
Lazy ctx resolver (lines 38-53): - Module-level `_ctx: ProjectContext | None = None` - `_get_project_context()` function caches on first call, lazy-resolves - Comment block explains: "Resolved lazily so import-time failures (missing registry, exotic cwd) don't break unit tests that exercise unrelated handlers." ✓ Excellent design preserving test isolation while enabling production path.
§D.1 list_tasks (lines 233-291): - Signature line 234: `def list_tasks(*, project_id: int, page: int = 1, ...)` ✓ keyword-only, REQUIRED, NO None default - Line 262: `_require_project_id(project_id)` guard ✓ - Line 275: `tasks = _api_get(f"/projects/{project_id}/tasks", params)` ✓ Always project-scoped path; the dangerous unscoped /tasks branch is REMOVED. - Docstring (lines 246-261) explicitly annotates §D.1 closure with rationale. ✓ §D.1 enforcement HARDENED. The "All tasks" leak is closed.
§D.1 search_tasks (lines 608-636): - Signature line 609: `def search_tasks(*, project_id: int, query: str)` ✓ keyword-only, REQUIRED, NO None default - Line 622: `_require_project_id(project_id)` guard ✓ - Lines 623-626: `_api_get(f"/projects/{project_id}/tasks", params={"s": query, ...})` ✓ Project-scoped path; the previously-default cross-project /tasks?s=... is REMOVED. - Docstring (lines 612-621) explicitly annotates §D.1 closure and notes "this was the more dangerous of the two -- search across-projects was previously default behavior." ✓ §D.1 enforcement HARDENED. The cross-project search bypass (more dangerous than list_tasks per EA-4's correct observation) is closed.
Defensive guards on 5 additional handlers (per EA-4 claim): - get_project (line 183): _require_project_id(project_id) ✓ - update_project (line 203): _require_project_id(project_id) ✓ - delete_project (line 225): _require_project_id(project_id) ✓ - create_task (line 313): _require_project_id(project_id) ✓ - bulk_create_tasks (line 654): _require_project_id(project_id) ✓ ✓ All 5 defensive guards confirmed. These extend the chokepoint contract beyond bare §D.1 scope (which targets list/search) but are well-judged defensive coding -- they reject bool/str/None project_id consistently, catching e.g. mistaken delete_project(True) calls before they hit Vikunja.
Handlers correctly NOT given guards (because they don't take project_id): list_projects, create_project, get_task, update_task, complete_task, delete_task, list_labels, create_label, add_label_to_task, remove_label_from_task, find_user, list_task_assignees, assign_user_to_task, remove_user_from_task, list_task_comments, add_task_comment, project_summary. ✓ Correctly scoped.
project_summary (line 677-698) note: This iterates per-project via /projects/{pid}/tasks per-call, which IS project-scoped. The /projects "all projects" listing is NOT a §D.1-violating tasks query. Acceptable.

git status does NOT show tools/fleet_ops/diff_builder.py as modified. EA-4 plan called for verify-only; verification passed (diff_builder tests pass per pytest 280-baseline). Correct adherence to v3 handoff "likely NO change" guidance.
git status does NOT show tools/autonomy_budget/active_tasks.py as modified. Verify-only per v3 handoff. Tests pass per pytest 280-baseline.

git status does NOT show tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py as modified. EA-4 correctly deferred to Item 2.6 per g5-4 disposition direction. The line-28 load_config callsite remains OPEN; per g5-4 + EA-4's iteration-1 ack, this MUST be CLOSED at 2.6 (either fold legacy file entirely or thin-shim with explicit ctx threading) before 2.9 single-commit. If 2.6 leaves it open, escalate to Guide before 2.9 commit (w15 hazard).

observation.topic: HTTP routing deferred -- guard imported directly

minor_observations_for_2.6_disposition.observation: EA-4's iteration-1 plan said for file 13: "(b) Refactor 19 MCP handlers to route through tools/_vikunja_client.VikunjaClient with _require_project_id guard." EA-4 did the second half (added _require_project_id guards) but NOT the first half (HTTP calls still go through httpx directly via _api_get / _api_put / _api_post / _api_delete; not through VikunjaClient instance methods). Defensible deferral because tools/_vikunja_client.py VikunjaClient HTTP delegation was intentionally stubbed at Stage 2.1 ("Delegation to the underlying HTTP client is wired in Stage 2.6"); routing through it now would require implementing the delegation first, which is out of v3-handoff 2.4 scope. The §D.1 hard rule (the security concern) IS closed at the handler level. Note for 2.6 disposition: when 2.6 routes call sites through VikunjaClient, the handler-level guards become redundant with VikunjaClient's internal _require_project_id call. EA-4's 2.6 work should decide: (a) remove handler-level guards (let VikunjaClient enforce), or (b) keep them as defense-in-depth. Either is fine; surface the choice in the 2.6 closure.

observation.topic: g5-3 disposition Option (b) — Guide endorses choice

minor_observations_for_2.6_disposition.observation: EA-4 chose Option (b) from the g5-3 disposition (thread ctx through to make fallback project-aware). The alternative was Option (a) (remove fallback if always-injected invariant is enforced). Option (b) is more conservative: it doesn't break any existing pattern, just adds a project-aware fallback path, while preserving the legacy zero-arg fallback for back-compat (README example, ad-hoc usage). Good engineering judgment. Guide endorses; no further action required.

observation.topic: r2 standing reminder over-corrected by EA-4 -- accepted

minor_observations_for_2.6_disposition.observation: EA-4 noted my standing reminder r2 (test_server_f5.py would need updating after signature change) was over-cautious because F-5 tests target label handlers, not list/search signatures. EA-4 is correct: the empirical pytest 280-pass result confirms no test in the current baseline depends on the old project_id=None default. My r2 was a precautionary what-if; EA-4's disk verification confirmed it didn't materialize. Accepted; no acknowledgment needed.

observation.topic: Pre-existing _get_token mypy str | None typing flagged but not introduced

minor_observations_for_2.6_disposition.observation: EA-4 noted: "Pre-existing: line 72 _get_token() mypy str | None → str is pre-existing, not introduced by this refactor." Confirmed by reading server.py: line 75-77 _get_token() returns `_token if _token is not None else _login()`; the function signature returns `str` but the return value is technically `str | None` per mypy because _token is module-level `str | None`. This is pre-existing technical debt, NOT introduced by EA-4's refactor. Out of 2.4 scope; can be addressed at Stage 6 hardening or as a separate ticket.

continue_or_handoff_recommendation.intro: EA-4 asked: "continue this session into 2.4.v2, or emit v4 handoff now while context is healthy and the file 13 closure is the clean breakpoint." Guide instance 5 recommendation below.

rationale.argument_for_continue:
2.4.v2 is verification (--help on each main()-bearing entrypoint + MCP handler routing check). Low-risk, fast, and EA-4 has the canonical pattern context loaded from the just-completed 13-file refactor. Splitting 2.4 from 2.4.v2 across sessions would force the next EA to re-audit all 13 files just to verify them. Inefficient.
2.5 (PS1 work + critical_notify.ps1 inspection) is self-contained scope with clear constraints from g4-1 + g4-2 + g4-3 acks. The canonical 7 PS1 enumeration is locked. The critical_notify.ps1 inspection rule is a single Appendix A.1 confirmation. Estimate: moderate complexity, smaller than file 13. EA-4 should be able to complete 2.5 + 2.5.v2 within remaining context budget given context was "healthy" after the largest single 2.4 lift.
rationale.argument_for_handoff_now:
2.6 is the next big-lift comparable to file 13: extended grep with 12 patterns (per iteration-2 gate) + chokepoint routing decisions across multiple files + g5-4 closure (legacy file fold-or-thin-shim disposition) + D3 closure (dashboard_maintainer.py:113 private-method abuse) + observation o1 (routing through VikunjaClient instance methods, which requires implementing VikunjaClient HTTP delegation first). 2.6 may itself take a focused session.
File 13 IS the natural breakpoint per D4 rationale -- it was the largest single lift in 2.4. Emitting v4 handoff at this clean boundary follows the same logic that drove EA-3's v3 handoff at the file 6/7 boundary.
rationale.synthesis:
The EA's own report frames file 13 closure as a "clean breakpoint" but also says "context is healthy." Both can be true. The optimal path is to push through 2.4.v2 (truly small) and 2.5 + 2.5.v2 (medium complexity, but smaller than file 13 individually and self-contained), then re-evaluate budget HONESTLY at the 2.5.v2 → 2.6 boundary. If budget is healthy at that boundary, push into 2.6 (and possibly 2.7 / 2.7.v2 / 2.8 / 2.9 / 2.10 if remaining capacity supports it). If even slightly tight, emit v4 mid-stage handoff per i1-4 / dn15 (docs-only commit, no partial-commit refactor). The "first sign of strain" framing EA-4 articulated in iteration-1 plan applies here.
The LA's instinct to continue is sound — context efficiency favors continuation when natural sub-scopes (verification + PS1 work) align with remaining capacity.

re_evaluation_criteria_at_2.5.v2_to_2.6_.criterion: Honestly assess remaining context budget. If percentage-consumed has crossed the threshold where you're tracking individual edits vs sessions, emit v4.

Look ahead to 2.6 work scope: extended grep + triage every hit + g5-4 closure decision + D3 closure + observation o1 (VikunjaClient HTTP delegation implementation if you choose to thread through it now). 2.6 is structurally as complex as file 13.
If unsure, emit v4. The v4 handoff cost (one docs-only commit + bootstrap cost for next EA) is small compared to the cost of a botched 2.6 mid-execution.
w13 single-commit discipline still applies: if you emit v4, it MUST be a docs-only commit. NO partial-commit refactor work folded in. NO emergency single-commit attempts that omit pending items.

guide_review_response.proceed_authorization: APPROVED -- EA-4's Item 2.4 file scope (files 7-13) is fully closed and ratified. CONTINUE into Item 2.4.v2 verification (--help on the main()-bearing entrypoints + MCP handler routing checks). After 2.4.v2 closes, proceed into Item 2.5 (canonical 7 PS1 with -BlarAIRoot per g4-2 + critical_notify.ps1 inspection per g4-1) and Item 2.5.v2 (per-PS1 verification). At the 2.5.v2 → 2.6 boundary, RE-EVALUATE budget honestly per the criteria above. Standing reminders applicable throughout: single Stage-2 commit at item 2.9 only (pyproject.toml MUST be in commit); fleet stays paused (NEVER state.resume_fleet — AttributeError); no merge to main; stash@{0} untouched; append-only STATUS at item 2.10; g5-4 legacy _vikunja_client.py:28 load_config callsite MUST be CLOSED at 2.6 disposition (escalate to Guide before 2.9 if 2.6 leaves it open); D3 dashboard_maintainer.py:113 private-method abuse closure at 2.6; observation o1 (handler-level guards vs VikunjaClient routing) is your 2.6 design choice. If context exhaustion threatens before item 2.9, emit v4 mid-stage handoff XML per i1-4 / dn15 (docs-only commit). On Stage 2 closure (item 2.10), emit your final closing handoff to LA per the closing_handoff_to_la_at_stage_2_close block in your initiation pointer.
